canonical_name: Egyptian non-Cretan correspondence corpus source-status + buildability audit (§III/§IV) → first mechanical INCOMPLETE verdict
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — design/source phase
research_question: Does any locally-available source yield a machine-readable, provenance-complete non-Cretan Egyptian→foreign correspondence corpus (Egyptian group-writing spelling ↔ independently-known foreign form, segment-aligned) fit to calibrate false-positive control?
method_family: source-availability / data-provenance audit feeding a mechanical status verdict
mechanical_verdict: verdict.py: status=INCOMPLETE, egyptian_channel_readiness=NOT_READY (load-bearing calibration corpus unbuildable; explicitly NOT a disguised NO_POWER)
data_sources_corpus_version: Hoch 1994 (sha 4df9bc09, 594pp, OCR-corrupt at transliteration layer); Kilani 2019 (sha 4ecff477, CC-BY, wrong layer=native vocalization); Kitchen NK Topo Lists (8pp, discussion only); Muchiki 1999 NOT ACQUIRED; Cretan/EA5647/Kom-el-Hetan EXCLUDED by rule
source_dependencies: Hoch 1994; Kilani 2019; Kitchen; Muchiki 1999 (absent) — all secondary/print Egyptological sources
effective_n_independence_unit: 0 admissible records (no corpus built); independence unit would be consonantal root
features_channels: intended: Egyptian group-writing grapheme ↔ Semitic consonant segment alignments + confidences
candidate_languages_hypotheses: NW/South/East/cross-Semitic (calibration side only); no Aegean claim
primary_metric_value: buildable=False; 0 admissible records — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (build_corpus()→[], 8 tests pass per DECISION_LOG E2..E3)
power: unassessable (no fittable-but-weak corpus exists) — explicitly distinguished from NO_POWER
strongest_authorized_claim: No standard-quality non-Cretan Egyptian→foreign correspondence corpus is buildable from available sources (source-access + extraction-defect blocker at load-bearing input)
forbidden_stronger_claim: Cannot claim the Egyptian channel has NO_POWER (no corpus was assessed) nor any correspondence/decipherment result
limitations_blockers: REQ-02b: needs machine-readable Hoch OR ≥150-entry hand-verified subset OR Muchiki 1999; auto-parsing corrupt OCR forbidden (would bake noise into FP calibration)
supersession_relations: SUPERSEDED by EGY-04 (Hoch OCR extraction, still blocked) then EGY-05 (vision-read corpus, blocker LIFTED)
verdict_ledger_reference: DECISION_LOG.md E1..E3 (2026-07-06); FINAL_EGYPTIAN_CHANNEL_VERDICT.md (INCOMPLETE pass, later overwritten by READY pass)

canonical_name: Calibration schema + inclusion rules + M0–M9 correspondence-model prereg spec freeze (§IV/§V, data-blind)
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — design freeze
research_question: Fix, before any data is viewed, the model families / regularization / cross-validation / acceptance thresholds so a later fit has no post-hoc design freedom
features_channels: planned: pooled probabilistic P(egyptian-grapheme | semitic-consonant), add-α smoothed; feature/λ grid frozen
algorithm_model: M0 identity baseline; M1 weighted edit-distance baseline; M2 pooled probabilistic correspondence; M3 names+toponyms; M4 toponyms-only; M5 period-matched; M6 genre-matched; M7 tier-A-only; M8 tier-C sensitivity; M9 permissive unconstrained (FP stress)
search_space_params: L2 λ grid via CV; grouped k-fold + leave-one-family/genre/period-out; seed 20260706; subgroup min n=30 else SUBGROUP_NO_POWER; acceptance = must beat M0/M1 held-out
preregistration_plan_hash: configs/egyptian_model_freeze.json sha256_16=3c56ed71 (design spec; NOT the operative estimator hash)
positive_negative_controls: planned: M9 permissive FP-null; baselines M0/M1
multiplicity_correction: acceptance thresholds + subgroup floor frozen ahead of data
contamination_leakage: spec pre-dates corpus (no leakage from validation results into design)
strongest_authorized_claim: The modeling plan is frozen data-blind; later fit carries no post-hoc design freedom
forbidden_stronger_claim: The spec (3c56ed71) is NOT the operative M2 estimator; the operative model.py hash is e76b2f231be8f0e6 — the freeze doc is provenance-only
supersession_relations: feeds EGY-06 (M2 fit). NOTE the operative estimator diverged from this aspirational M0–M9 doc (prereg §inputs flags the design spec as 'aspirational … NOT the operative estimator')

canonical_name: Hoch-1994 OCR auto-extraction of the calibration corpus (extract_hoch.py)
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — corpus-build attempt (OCR route)
research_question: Can the load-bearing correspondence corpus be auto-derived from the owned Hoch 1994 scan's OCR text layer to the ≥150 clean-entry standard?
mechanical_verdict: verdict UNCHANGED: INCOMPLETE / NOT_READY (auto-derived corpus noise-limited below threshold; forbidden to clean via circular similarity heuristic)
data_sources_corpus_version: Hoch 1994 corrupt OCR body layer (Princeton UP, copyrighted; derived data gitignored)
effective_n_independence_unit: 76 tier-A candidate pairs (below ≥150); 101 entries parsed; 25 tier-C — REPORT_ONLY (derived data gitignored)
features_channels: Egyptian headword (extracts cleanly, aleph→'3'/ayin→'c') + Semitic source-form pairing (NOT reliably recoverable)
segmentation: entry-block boundary detection (bleeds; multiple comparanda caught)
primary_metric_value: 101 entries / 76 tier-A / 25 tier-C — REPORT_ONLY (extractor+counts committed, data gitignored)
uncertainty: pairing errors (abu✓, atpata✓, aswata✗ bled from next entry, Yamsula✗) — headword↔etymon un-cleanable without hand-verification
contamination_leakage: similarity-based pairing FORBIDDEN (would bias the very correspondence probabilities = circular fishing-rod the gate exists to prevent)
strongest_authorized_claim: The corpus is partially derivable but not to the non-circular standard a calibration requires without hand-verification or Muchiki 1999
forbidden_stronger_claim: Cannot use the ~76 auto-paired candidates as a calibration corpus (circular / noise-limited)
limitations_blockers: needs clean machine-readable Hoch OR Muchiki 1999 OR authorized hand-verification pass
supersession_relations: SUPERSEDED by EGY-04 (vision-read of page images bypasses OCR)

canonical_name: Vision-reading of Hoch 1994 page images → hand-verified calibration corpus (REQ-02 blocker LIFTED)
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — corpus build (vision route)
research_question: Can the corpus be derived to standard by reading Hoch's page IMAGES directly (bypassing corrupt OCR), using Hoch's own [1]–[5] reliability tags for tiering?
method_family: vision transcription / hand-verification of a print source
mechanical_verdict: gate status: INCOMPLETE(no corpus) → CORPUS DERIVED / READY-TO-FIT; sha256 cc2c20d8 frozen
data_sources_corpus_version: Hoch 1994 page images (PDF p.36–247), author-tiered; frozen egyptian_calibration_handverified.jsonl sha256 cc2c20d8 (gitignored, copyrighted); manifest committed
effective_n_independence_unit: 156 distinct consonantal roots (effective independent units) from 159 records; 152 tier-A/B (99 A, 53 B) — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (manifest sha cc2c20d8; model_validation.json n=152)
features_channels: Egyptian group-writing consonantal skeleton ↔ romanized Semitic cognate(s) + language tag + Hoch reliability tag
segmentation: consonantal-skeleton dedup (e.g. four n-h-r → one) ≈ 1 record/root
candidate_languages_hypotheses: cognate spread BH 142 / Ug 84 / Akk 64 / Ar 40 / Aram 30 / Ph 25 / Syr 24 / Amor 9 / Eth 4 (REPORT_ONLY)
heldout_design: later skeleton-collision holdout applied downstream (159→123 records)
primary_metric_value: 159 records; 152 tier-A/B; 156 roots; item classes LOANWORD 130 / PERSONAL_NAME 23 / DIVINE_NAME 3 / TOPONYM 1 / ETHNONYM 1 / OTHER 1 — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (manifest + report)
uncertainty: transcription reliability captured via Hoch's own tags
power: adequate to fit (≥150 tier-A/B threshold met)
contamination_leakage: Cretan material excluded by construction (Hoch is Semitic); zero leakage
licence_earned: NONE (a calibration corpus, not a transfer licence)
strongest_authorized_claim: A provenance-complete, author-tiered, root-independent non-Cretan calibration corpus now exists to standard
forbidden_stronger_claim: The corpus proves nothing about Linear A / Cretan; it only enables FP calibration of the Egyptian channel
limitations_blockers: reproduce requires re-reading the copyrighted owned scan (derived JSON gitignored)
supersession_relations: LIFTS the EGY-01/EGY-03 blocker; enables EGY-05..EGY-10

canonical_name: M2 pooled-probabilistic correspondence model fit + held-out validation (§VI)
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — model fit/validation
research_question: Does a correspondence model learned only on non-Cretan Semitic data beat identity/edit-distance baselines out-of-sample and per family?
method_family: probabilistic grapheme-correspondence model (add-α positional alignment) with LOO/leave-one-family-out validation
effective_n_independence_unit: n=152 records / 149 distinct candidates; independence unit = consonantal root/family
features_channels: pooled P(egyptian-grapheme | semitic-consonant), add-α=0.5, best-alignment scoring of candidate source skeletons
algorithm_model: M2 correspondence (operative model.py sha e76b2f231be8f0e6) vs M0 identity, M1 edit-distance baselines
candidate_languages_hypotheses: Semitic families (calibration); learned l→r merger, aleph→Ⱥ, b→b/p devoicing
heldout_design: leave-one-out top-1 + leave-one-family-out; East Semitic n_test=1 → SUBGROUP_NO_POWER
primary_metric_value: M2 top-1 0.691 (top5 0.855, MRR 0.753) vs M0/M1 top-1 0.434 (MRR 0.562); LOFO NW 0.767 (n=30) / South 0.667 (n=3) / cross 0.602 (n=118) — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (results/model_validation.json)
power: adequate at n=152; East subgroup NO_POWER
ablations_robustness: sensitivity to transcription uncertainty handled in EGY-08
contamination_leakage: out-of-sample (LOO/LOFO); learned correspondences match published Egyptology (l→r), not noise
licence_earned: NONE (calibration-internal; no transfer licence to Aegean)
strongest_authorized_claim: The frozen correspondence model beats simple baselines out-of-sample and per family on non-Cretan Semitic data
forbidden_stronger_claim: No Aegean/Linear A phonetic or lexical validity implied; a within-Semitic fit does not license cross-script transfer
limitations_blockers: East Semitic subgroup unpowered; validity confined to the calibration corpus
supersession_relations: feeds EGY-06..EGY-09 gate stages and EGY-12 one-shot

canonical_name: Matched-scarcity Linear-B positive control (§VII) — recovery at Cretan-anchor scarcity
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — positive control
research_question: Does the validated model retain recovery at the few-short-anchor scarcity of the intended Cretan test, and what is the minimum detectable anchor count?
data_sources_corpus_version: calibration corpus cc2c20d8 (recovery by anchor length)
effective_n_independence_unit: per-length n: len2 n=11, len3 n=116, len4 n=22, len5 n=3
features_channels: LOO top-1 recovery stratified by anchor consonant-length
search_space_params: K anchor sweep K=2..6; short-form = len≤4
positive_negative_controls: this IS the positive control; per-anchor null 1/149≈0.0067
multiplicity_correction: expected-recovered vs null P(≥1) per K
primary_metric_value: recovery len2 0.818 / len3 0.638 / len4 0.864 / len5 1.0; short-form (≤4) 0.685; per-anchor null 0.00671; min_detectable_anchors=2; expected@K3=2.05 vs null P(≥1)=0.020 — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (results/gate.json)
power: min detectable 2 anchors; P(NO_POWER at K=3)=0.031 (see EGY-09)
contamination_leakage: LOO; DAMOS Linear B as the readable-analog control
strongest_authorized_claim: At the intended Cretan scarcity (few, short anchors) the model recovers ~0.68 ≫ ~0.007 null; a 2-anchor test is detectable
forbidden_stronger_claim: Does not show Cretan/Aegean recovery works; only that the calibrated instrument has power at that scarcity
limitations_blockers: recovery on Semitic ≠ recovery on Aegean ovals
supersession_relations: companion to EGY-05/EGY-07/EGY-08/EGY-09; feeds EGY-10 verdict

canonical_name: End-to-end null family — random-pairing / permuted-model / permissive-M9 (§VIII)
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — negative controls / specificity
research_question: Does the recovery signal live in the real learned correspondences, or would broken/permissive procedures recover just as well (excessive false positives)?
mechanical_verdict: specificity_ok=true; permissive_excessive_fp=false; search architecture NOT rejected (gate.py)
effective_n_independence_unit: n=152 (LOO top-1 under each broken procedure)
features_channels: recovery under (1) random pairing, (2) permuted egy↔sem link refit, (3) permissive all-substitutions-free M9
preregistration_plan_hash: frozen spec 3c56ed71 (model/threshold/family prespecified; best-of-model covered by permissive null)
positive_negative_controls: these ARE the negative controls; real M2 0.691 is the reference
multiplicity_correction: permissive M9 covers best-of-model selection (no load-bearing adaptive choice outside the null)
primary_metric_value: real M2 0.691 vs random 0.000 / permuted 0.1184 / permissive M9 0.0066 — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (results/gate.json.nulls). Note report rounds permissive to 0.007
contamination_leakage: nulls destroy the real correspondence, isolating signal
strongest_authorized_claim: The recovery signal is specific to the real learned correspondences; a shuffled or permissive model recovers near chance
forbidden_stronger_claim: Specificity on Semitic does not license any Aegean claim

research_question: Does the readiness verdict reverse under plausible Egyptian-rendering/sign-value transcription uncertainty?
effective_n_independence_unit: n=152; per-anchor perturbations of k consonants
features_channels: top-1 + short-recovery under LOW/CENTRAL/HIGH consonant perturbation
search_space_params: LOW none; CENTRAL ~30% anchors 1 consonant; HIGH every anchor 1 consonant
primary_metric_value: top-1 LOW 0.691 / CENTRAL 0.586 / HIGH 0.349; short-recovery LOW 0.685 / CENTRAL 0.577 / HIGH 0.342 (≈50× null) — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (results/gate.json.transcription_uncertainty)
strongest_authorized_claim: The readiness verdict is robust to plausible transcription/sign-value uncertainty (no reversal)
forbidden_stronger_claim: Robustness on Semitic recovery does not extend to Aegean readings

canonical_name: Egyptian-channel power envelope (§X) — minimum useful anchor count
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — power sizing
research_question: How many short Cretan anchors are needed to detect recovery above the null, and what is P(NO_POWER) at the expected K=3?
mechanical_verdict: min useful anchors=2; P(NO_POWER at K=3)=0.031 (gate.py)
features_channels: expected recovered vs null 95th pct across K
primary_metric_value: min useful anchors=2; P(NO_POWER at K=3 short)=0.031; recovery by length len2 0.818…len5 1.0 — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (results/gate.json.power)
uncertainty: power derived from frozen (not searched) correspondences (permuted null 0.118, permissive 0.007 confirm)
strongest_authorized_claim: With ~3 short anchors the design almost never fails to recover ≥1 (P NO_POWER 0.031)
forbidden_stronger_claim: Power to detect ≠ evidence that a Cretan reading is correct
limitations_blockers: assumes Aegean recovery mirrors Semitic recovery (untested until one-shot)

canonical_name: Mechanical gate readiness verdict (§XI) → COMPLETE / READY_FOR_PREREG_DRAFT
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — final verdict
research_question: Given corpus+model+control+nulls+robustness+power, is the Egyptian channel ready to draft a preregistered one-shot Cretan-anchor test?
features_channels: 10 boolean criteria (corpus valid, beats baselines, family-robust, deterministic, control pass, FP acceptable, no adaptive choice, held-out adequate, uncertainty no-reversal, REQ-01 unresolved flag)
candidate_languages_hypotheses: Aegean/pre-Greek target flagged CONFIRMATORY_INELIGIBLE pending REQ-01
primary_metric_value: key_numbers: tier_A_B 152, M2_top1 0.691, baseline_top1 0.434, short_recovery 0.685, min_detectable_anchors 2, null_random 0.0, null_permuted 0.1184, HIGH_uncertainty_recovery 0.342, P_no_power_K3 0.031 — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (results/final_verdict.json)
uncertainty: readiness = DESIGN readiness, not a decipherment
contamination_leakage: Cretan targets flagged CONFIRMATORY_INELIGIBLE until REQ-01 primary edition collated
strongest_authorized_claim: The non-Cretan calibration is powered and FP-controlled; draft a preregistration for a later one-shot Cretan test
forbidden_stronger_claim: READY_FOR_PREREG_DRAFT means 'draft the prereg', NOT 'the reading works'; no Linear A phonetic/lexical/language-family/decipherment claim, no Cretan correspondence
limitations_blockers: Cretan confirmatory targets ineligible until REQ-01 (Edel & Görg 2005 / Kitchen collation)
supersession_relations: READINESS is DESIGN-level; the later one-shot (EGY-13) returned honest NULL, closing the phonetic-anchor programme. STALE-STATUS FLAG: STATUS.md still headlines 'READY_FOR_PREREG_DRAFT / first channel to pass' and DECISION_LOG stops at E6, neither reflecting the EGY-13 TRIVIAL/NULL downgrade

canonical_name: REQ-01 Aegean-list apparatus determination via Bělohoubková 2014 thesis (candidate target set)
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — confirmatory target sourcing
research_question: Can the Kom el-Hetan En-list Cretan toponym readings be frozen as confirmatory targets from available (secondary) sources, cross-checked ≥2 independent sources?
data_sources_corpus_version: Bělohoubková 2014 BA thesis (sha 91a0035b, vision-read); cross-check Cline & Stannish 2011 + Edel & Görg 2005 via German Wikipedia reproduction
source_dependencies: Bělohoubková 2014 (secondary); C&S 2011; Edel & Görg 2005 (only via WP reproduction at this stage)
effective_n_independence_unit: 3 freezable-candidate toponyms (Knossos/Amnisos/Lyktos); 2 not freezable (Phaistos SUBSTANTIVE_DISCREPANCY, Kydonia single-authority)
features_channels: normalized consonantal skeleton + En list position + Linear B identification
algorithm_model: workflow wf_0d343956-f7c (9 agents, 0 errors), ≥2-source agreement rule
search_space_params: normalization: aleph/glides dropped; Gardiner M8 s3/S3/SA/š3→š
candidate_languages_hypotheses: Aegean toponyms (Linear B Greek): Knossos k-n-š, Amnisos m-n-š, Lyktos r-k-t
positive_negative_controls: ≥2 independent-source agreement as the admission control
multiplicity_correction: 6-oval list reduced to 3 by mechanical rule (deflation acknowledged)
primary_metric_value: 3 FREEZABLE(candidate) / 2 NOT-freezable — REPORT_ONLY (from thesis + WP cross-check; per-agent apparatus in wf journal)
uncertainty: reproduced plates (Obr.5=Taf.13, Obr.7=Taf.14) too small/low-contrast to resolve to sign level
ablations_robustness: Lyktos independence rests on C&S 2011 (thesis+WP both trace to Edel&Görg 2005:188 = one source)
contamination_leakage: reading-independence caveat: Edel read eroded ovals using Greek IDs → skeleton not cleanly independent of the answer (§15.6)
strongest_authorized_claim: Knossos/Amnisos/Lyktos are a pre-registerable CANDIDATE target set; not confirmatory until direct primary collation
forbidden_stronger_claim: Facsimile reproduction inside a secondary source is NOT personal collation (Invariant 3); do NOT freeze Phaistos/Kydonia
limitations_blockers: needs direct collation of Edel & Görg 2005 plates (Tf.13/14) or Kitchen's full collation at sign-level resolution
supersession_relations: SUPERSEDED by EGY-12 (primary edition delivered + collated, REQ-01 CLOSED)

canonical_name: REQ-01 direct collation of Edel & Görg 2005 primary edition → frozen Cretan confirmatory targets (3→5)
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — confirmatory target freeze
research_question: Does direct collation of the primary edition (Edel & Görg 2005, ÄAT 50) confirm and freeze the Cretan En-list toponym targets to confirmatory-eligible standard?
data_sources_corpus_version: Edel & Görg 2005 (ÄAT 50, user-delivered scp, 20MB, 135pp OCR scan, sha 2a6f7f19, gitignored); frozen targets sha256 6903f5f1 (5 targets). MDAIK 60 (2004) registered CONTEXT_ONLY (does not lift §15.6)
source_dependencies: Edel & Görg 2005 primary edition; Osing autopsy; Kitchen 1965
effective_n_independence_unit: 5 frozen targets (3 FROZEN + 2 FROZEN_WITH_ALTERNATIVES); +1 secondary robustness anchor Kythera
features_channels: Edel oval transliteration + apparatus + certainty qualifiers + Görg (MG:) revisions + line-drawings
search_space_params: skeleton-blind selection rule; certainty tiers sicher / sehr gut / fraglich
candidate_languages_hypotheses: Aegean toponyms: Knossos ka-jn-jw-ša=knš, Amnisos [j]ʿ-m-n-i-ša=ʾmnš, Lyktos rj-ka-tj=rkt, Phaistos ba-y-ša-tj=byšt, Kydonia Ka-tw-na-y=ktny
positive_negative_controls: competing readings carried as alternatives, not collapsed to a cherry-picked choice
multiplicity_correction: targets externally fixed by the ancient list (not tuned)
primary_metric_value: 3→5 anchors (Phaistos/Kydonia overturned from not-freezable to freezable via Osing autopsy); Lyktos L→Egyptian r matches the model's learned P(egy|l):r — REPORT_ONLY (collation, targets json sha 6903f5f1)
uncertainty: plate verification outstanding (Tafel 12 too eroded; Falttafeln 13/14 hand-copies ABSENT from scan); freeze rests on Edel edition text + his line-drawings; aleph/ayin printed identically (Amnisos leading sign ambiguous)
ablations_robustness: Phaistos/Kydonia palimpsest-dependent (original li14/li15 ovals physically lost)
contamination_leakage: reading-independence §15.6 NOT fully resolved: Falttafeln 13/14 unavailable; claim scoped to 'recovers Edel's ID-informed readings'
strongest_authorized_claim: The Cretan confirmatory target set is frozen from the primary edition; the one-shot preregistration is UNBLOCKED
forbidden_stronger_claim: No Linear A matching / sign-value claim / external preregistration; targets must never enter Linear A hypothesis formation
limitations_blockers: sign-level plate verification (Falttafeln 13/14) still outstanding; full reading-independence unproven
supersession_relations: SUPERSEDES EGY-11 candidate set (ADVANCED_PROXIMATE_ONLY → CLOSED); enables EGY-13

canonical_name: Cretan-anchor one-shot confirmatory preregistration (mint) + red-team hardening (plan_hash 2eab1536)
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — one-shot preregistration
research_question: Freeze, before running, the one-shot test: does the non-Cretan-fit M2 recover Cretan LB toponyms at top-1 above scramble AND above both a model-free baseline and a generic-Egyptian ablation?
mechanical_verdict: status=FROZEN_MINTED; plan_hash 2eab1536cf70c20d0faebafff813b190b9cea573433698b2fdc2d83825cd130a; supersedes withdrawn v1 (red-team wf_3bfac2b3-fdf → REVISE_THEN_COMMIT)
data_sources_corpus_version: post-holdout corpus egyptian_calibration_heldout.jsonl sha 612ff9e9 (159→123 records, 117 tier-A/B, skeleton-collision holdout); candidate pool 981 DAMOS wordform skeletons (content sha 26eb76278f7a9b65)
source_dependencies: EGY-04 corpus, EGY-05 model (operative model.py sha e76b2f23), EGY-12 frozen targets, DAMOS Linear B pool
effective_n_independence_unit: K=3 anchors (Knossos/Amnisos/Lyktos); deflation 6→3 by mechanical rule
features_channels: one deterministic hash-pinned skeletonizer f (sha feb7e8dc) on oval / target LB / pool; aleph/ayin/glides dropped both sides; š vs s kept distinct
algorithm_model: M2 vs B_id (identity/edit-distance/consonant-multiset) + B_egy (generic Egyptian sign→consonant, no Semitic fit); joint Monte-Carlo permutation N≥10000
search_space_params: pool M=981; NO_POWER if M<30 or <3 distractors within edit-1 or P_null(r1≥2)≥0.05; decision rules REFUTE/RECOVERED_TRIVIAL/CONFIRM_GENERALIZES pre-specified
segmentation: single f() governs all three scoring surfaces
candidate_languages_hypotheses: Aegean / pre-Greek (Linear B Greek); confidence_prior 0.4; downstream cap 0.75 non-compounding
heldout_design: skeleton-collision holdout → zero target-skeleton information; pool built blind to scores; as_of precedes run (fail-closed)
positive_negative_controls: B_id, B_egy ablations + scramble family (random_pairing, permuted_model); no-pilot attestation (verified wf_80d0808d-b95)
null_family_run_count: joint permutation null N=10000; scramble family 2 variants
multiplicity_correction: 6→3 anchor deflation; Phaistos/Kydonia/Kythera + full 6-oval list = SYMMETRIC secondary (a secondary FAIL counts against)
primary_metric_value: design targets frozen; input hashes recorded (target b749e2f7, model e76b2f23, skeletonizer feb7e8dc, verdict-script 3cc8a8b2, pool ff83993c) — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (prereg json)
uncertainty: reading-independence leak documented per anchor (§15.6, Falttafeln absent) — claim scoped to 'recovers Edel's readings'
power: endpoint-clearability: realistic r1=2 must reach p<0.05 (else NO_POWER)
ablations_robustness: v1 withdrawn after red-team; v2 hardened; REDTEAM response committed
contamination_leakage: corpus exclusion by SKELETON not label; firewall: Cretan targets never enter Linear A hypothesis formation/scoring
strongest_authorized_claim: A fail-closed one-shot plan is minted; the run may proceed exactly once with no retune
forbidden_stronger_claim: The design spec 3c56ed71 is NOT the operative estimator; any deviation voids plan_hash → fresh prereg
limitations_blockers: PRE-RUN FLAW (identified only post-run, EGY-14): baseline set omitted a fold-tolerant generic-Egyptian baseline; B_egy under-specified as strict char-identity
supersession_relations: supersedes withdrawn v1; consumed by EGY-14 (run); v3 lesson recorded (needs sibilant-fold baseline + support>n=1 + independent-correspondence anchors)

canonical_name: Cretan-anchor one-shot confirmatory RUN — mechanical CONFIRM_GENERALIZES vs honest RECOVERED_TRIVIAL / NULL
campaign: Egyptian external-anchor calibration gate — one-shot confirmatory test
research_question: Does the frozen non-Cretan-fit M2 correspondence model generalize to recover the Cretan LB toponyms above scramble AND above the model-free/generic-Egyptian baselines (i.e. is the Egyptian→Aegean channel real)?
method_family: one-shot preregistered confirmatory test + 5-agent adversarial verification
mechanical_verdict: pre-registered mechanical verdict = CONFIRM_GENERALIZES (stands, no retune, invariant 3); HONEST interpretation = RECOVERED_TRIVIAL / NULL (adversarial adjudication DOWNGRADE_TO_TRIVIAL, result_stands=false)
data_sources_corpus_version: post-holdout corpus sha 612ff9e9; pool M=981 DAMOS wordforms; 3 anchors from frozen targets sha 6903f5f1
source_dependencies: EGY-13 prereg (plan_hash 2eab1536); operative model.py e76b2f23; EGY-05 M2; EGY-12 targets
effective_n_independence_unit: nominal K=3; EFFECTIVE evidence ≈1 (Lyktos = pure-identity freebie every baseline gets; Knossos+Amnisos hinge on the SAME terminal š→s rule learned from exactly ONE training pair HV-0234)
features_channels: M2 vs B_id vs B_egy on blind f()-skeletons; per-anchor ranks over 981-pool
algorithm_model: M2 correspondence + joint permutation null N=10000
candidate_languages_hypotheses: Aegean / pre-Greek (Linear B Greek); Knossos k-n-š→k-n-s, Amnisos m-n-š→m-n-s, Lyktos r-k-t→r-k-t
heldout_design: Lev≤1 skeleton-collision holdout (real; HV-0234 legitimately survives, its skeleton p-s-l is Lev≥2 from every target)
positive_negative_controls: B_id, B_egy; permutation null histogram; 5-agent red-team (wf_7ddc21a2-027, all four lenses returned ARTIFACT)
null_family_run_count: 1 joint permutation null, N=10000 (null r1 hist {0:9062,1:881,2:44,3:13})
multiplicity_correction: 6→3 anchor deflation; per-anchor confusable-within-1 pool depth reported
primary_metric_value: r1_M2=3/3, r1_B_id=1, r1_B_egy=1, p=0.0014 (surprisal 9.48 bits); per-anchor Knossos rank_M2 1/B_id 2/B_egy 10, Amnisos 1/2/13, Lyktos 1/1/1 — VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (results/cretan_oneshot_verdict.json)
uncertainty: LOO of the single record HV-0234 flips verdict 3→1 (REFUTE); a fair sibilant-fold generic-Egyptian baseline also reaches 3/3 (ties M2 with less info) → prereg's own RECOVERED_TRIVIAL branch fires
power: p-value establishes non-randomness ONLY; does NOT test robustness to M2's own support (LOO flips) nor M2 vs a fair fold baseline (ties)
ablations_robustness: adversarial verification: fair baseline ties; edge=1 record; effective-n≈1; mechanism mislabel (Egyptian scribal orthography of foreign sibilants, NOT Semitic–Aegean phonology); homophony (k-n-s shared by 7 DAMOS wordforms)
contamination_leakage: no bug/leak/preprocessing cheat (harness honest); Edel read final sign as š ≠ answer s → MITIGATES the §15.6 reading-circularity worry
licence_earned: NONE (downstream vocalization / Linear A probe / sound-JEPA NOT licensed; ≤0.75 cap bounds propagation but does not rescue a mislabeled positive)
strongest_authorized_claim: The Egyptian rendering convention for foreign /s/ recurs on the Cretan ovals — an honest NULL; the channel is NOT shown to generalize (the insurance-policy outcome)
forbidden_stronger_claim: NEVER 'a Semitic-trained correspondence generalizes to Aegean phonology'; NEVER 'ranked all 3 Cretan toponyms at top-1'; do NOT cite p=0.0014 as evidence of non-trivial generalization; do NOT use the unqualified 3/3 language
limitations_blockers: phonetic-anchor programme CLOSED; v3 would need (a) sibilant-fold generic-Egyptian baseline, (b) support>n=1 per load-bearing correspondence, (c) anchors exercising INDEPENDENT correspondences not the same rule twice
supersession_relations: supersedes the EGY-10 READY optimism in substance; the mechanical CONFIRM stands as pre-registered record while the interpretation is corrected to NULL (append-only, no retune). CONTRADICTS stale STATUS.md/DECISION_LOG which stop at READY_FOR_PREREG_DRAFT
verdict_ledger_reference: results/cretan_oneshot_verdict.json (verdict CONFIRM_GENERALIZES); CRETAN_ANCHOR_RESULT.md (honest RECOVERED_TRIVIAL/NULL); red-team wf_7ddc21a2-027
